task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Crew View app is open and shows a search query or flight status results for American Airlines flight 1 (AA 1).
The Flight Crew View app shows a search query or flight status results for United Airlines flight 1 (UA 1).

task_completion.description: Search for Delta Air Lines flight 1 (DL 1)
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Flight Crew View app shows a search query or flight status results for Delta Air Lines flight 1 (DL 1).

task_completion.description: Open a text editor or spreadsheet app to create the CSV
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or spreadsheet application is open on the screen, ready for text input.
The exact text 'Flight,Origin,Destination,Status' is visible as the first line or header row in the editor.

task_completion.description: Write the flight data rows for the three flights
task_completion.visual_evidence: Three rows of data are visible below the header, corresponding to AA 1, UA 1, and DL 1, containing their respective Origin, Destination, and Status (or 'Not Found' if applicable).

task_completion.description: Save the file as flight_report.csv in the /sdcard/ directory
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog, file manager, or editor title bar shows the file being saved with the name 'flight_report.csv' in the '/sdcard/' directory.

integrity.description: Agent used the Flight Crew View app GUI to look up flights
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the actual Flight Crew View Android app interface, not a terminal, script, or web browser.

integrity.description: Agent used a GUI application to create the CSV file
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a text editor or file manager GUI being used to write and save the CSV, rather than terminal commands like 'echo'.

integrity.description: Agent entered genuine data retrieved from the app
integrity.visual_evidence: The flight data (Origin, Destination, Status) entered into the CSV matches the information that was displayed on the screen during the app searches, proving the agent didn't hallucinate or hardcode the answers.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected output file must be saved at '/sdcard/flight_report.csv'. The required CSV header is exactly 'Flight,Origin,Destination,Status'. The agent must look up three specific flights: AA 1 (American Airlines), UA 1 (United Airlines), and DL 1 (Delta Air Lines). Real-world routes for these are AA 1 (JFK-LAX), UA 1 (SFO-SIN), and DL 1 (JFK-LHR), but the VLM should verify that the CSV data matches whatever the app actually displays. If a flight is not found in the app, the corresponding data fields in the CSV must be recorded as 'Not Found'.
